passages_en: Laser wire-filled welding of thin aluminum alloy sheet was performed with a set laser power of 2.8 kW, welding speed of 1.2 m/min, shielding gas Ar, and porosity defects were observed in the weld bead.
304 stainless steel butt welding: the process card specifies 3.0 kW power; measured weld width was insufficient with accompanying undercut.
6061-T6 sheet PA welding with ER4043 filler wire at 2.5 kW; spatter anomaly was not annotated in the gold standard.
Duplex stainless steel laser welding: elevated heat input caused coarse grain growth; the gold standard only records lack-of-fusion risk.
Titanium alloy seal welding: power window 1.8–2.2 kW, current setpoint 2.0 kW, surface oxidation color normal.
Galvanized steel laser brazing at 1.6 kW laser power; weld formation was good with no defects detected.
Nickel-based superalloy cladding with He shielding gas; excessive interpass temperature caused crack susceptibility.
Cu-Al dissimilar metal laser welding at 4.2 kW; brittle intermetallic at the interface caused delamination.
High-strength steel laser keyhole welding at 5.0 kW with CO2 shielding gas; spatter and porosity coexisted.
Magnesium alloy laser welding at 1.2 kW; inadequate oxide removal led to lack of fusion.
